promoBar.text: Free drone field analysis for SLO County farms — crop health maps, terrain models & thermal scans.

hero.sub: Serving San Luis Obispo County vineyards, row crops, and orchards. Same-week scheduling. Zero soil compaction.

heroPromoItems.title: Field Health Map
heroPromoItems.desc: Spots disease, pest pressure & nutrient gaps weeks before they're visible on foot

heroPromoItems.title: Terrain & Drainage Map
heroPromoItems.desc: Shows where water pools, runs off, or bypasses crops so you can fix it

heroPromoItems.title: Thermal Irrigation Check
heroPromoItems.desc: Finds dry zones and failing emitters before they cost you yield

services.title: Crop Spraying
services.tagline: Precise aerial application over any terrain
bullets: Fungicides, herbicides & pesticides
Consistent coverage at variable rates
Access to wet or soft fields
Reduced chemical drift
GPS-guided flight paths

services.title: Cover Crop Seeding
bullets: Seed before harvest to maximize growth window
No soil disturbance or compaction
Uniform broadcast distribution
Works in tight canopy conditions
Faster than ground equipment

services.title: Frost Seeding
bullets: Seed clovers & grasses in late winter
Freeze-thaw cycle works seed into soil
No equipment in muddy fields
Earlier establishment than drill seeding
Cost-effective legume establishment

farmerPromo.headline: Spot Problems Before They Do Damage
farmerPromo.sub: We're offering a complimentary drone survey to a limited number of farms in San Luis Obispo County this season. No cost, no obligation — just actionable data to help you get ahead of problems before the season gets away from you.
farmerPromo.ctaNote: We'll confirm your spot and schedule a time that works around your operation.

services.label: Crop Health
services.title: Field Health Map
bullets: High-res aerial mosaic of your entire field in one pass
Reveals disease, pest pressure & nutrient deficiency
Stress shows up weeks before it's visible on foot
GPS-tagged problem zones you can walk straight to

services.label: Irrigation & Planning
services.title: Terrain & Drainage Map
services.tagline: See where water flows — and where it doesn't
bullets: Precise elevation model of every acre
Shows where water pools, runs off fast, or bypasses crops
Guides irrigation zone layout and tile drain decisions
Useful for planting configuration and harvest planning

services.label: Water Stress Detection
services.title: Thermal Irrigation Check
services.tagline: Find your dry spots before yield is lost
bullets: Thermal imaging reveals heat-stressed zones across the field
Finds clogged emitters and failing irrigation sectors
Plants heat up days before any visible wilting
Saves you hours of manual field-walking to find problems

process.title: Request a Quote
process.desc: Fill out our quick form with your acreage, crop type, and target application.

process.title: Field Assessment
process.desc: We review your field layout, obstacles, and schedule a flight window.

process.title: Schedule Service
process.desc: We confirm a date — often same week — and coordinate product delivery.

process.title: Application Day
process.desc: We fly, apply, and send you a completion report with flight data.

pricing.note: $400 minimum per job · Product not included · Ferry fees may apply beyond 50 miles

faq.q: What is the minimum job size?
faq.a: We have a $400 minimum per job, which covers up to ~33 acres at our base rate. Smaller fields are welcome — you just pay the minimum.

faq.q: Can you fly in wet or muddy conditions?
faq.a: Yes — that's one of the biggest advantages of drone application. As long as it's not actively raining and winds are under 15 mph, we can fly when ground equipment is stuck.
No, product cost is separate. We work with your agronomist or co-op to coordinate delivery, or you can supply it directly. We handle loading, calibration, and application.

faq.q: How far in advance do I need to book?
faq.a: We typically schedule within the same week, sometimes same day for urgent applications. Book early during peak fungicide season (late June–July) as slots fill fast.
Yes. We hold an FAA Part 107 Remote Pilot Certificate and carry full commercial liability insurance. Applicator certification details available on request.
Our primary fleet is DJI Agras series — high-volume agricultural drones purpose-built for spray applications with RTK GPS precision.

testimonials.text: Had standing water in half my cornfield and couldn't get the sprayer in. They were out the next day and covered everything. Saved the fungicide timing.
testimonials.author: Mike R.

testimonials.text: Used them for cover crop seeding into soybeans. Germination was excellent and I didn't have to wait until after harvest. Will be doing it every year.
testimonials.author: Tom K.

testimonials.text: Straightforward pricing, showed up on time, sent me a flight log after. Exactly what you want from a service provider.
testimonials.author: Janet L.

promoLanding.headline: Spot Problems Before They Do Damage
promoLanding.sub: A complimentary three-part drone survey for select SLO County farms — field health maps, terrain models, and thermal irrigation scans. No cost, no commitment.
promoLanding.whyHeadline: Why are we doing this for free?
promoLanding.whyBody: We're new and want to show local farmers what precision drone imaging can actually do — not with a brochure, but with real data from your fields. If you find it useful, we hope you'll think of us when it's time for spraying or seeding. If not, no hard feelings.
promoLanding.whyBody2: We're limiting this to a small number of farms so we can do each one properly. Once spots are gone, they're gone.
